export.common.no_redundancy: ensure replication or backup is configured.
export.common.single_node_note: Single node — no failover redundancy. Acceptable for a DR tier that is itself the redundancy; not recommended for production clusters.
export.docx.aime_bullet_1: Reduce manual intervention — comprehensive monitoring, proactive problem detection, and automated remediation keep the cluster healthy.
export.docx.aime_bullet_2: Simplified troubleshooting — precise problem determination and actionable insights replace the guesswork of log interpretation.
export.docx.aime_bullet_3: Autonomous remediation — automatically addresses issues to minimize downtime.
export.docx.aime_bullet_4: Predictive anomaly detection — continuous monitoring surfaces problems before they escalate.
export.docx.aime_bullet_5: Zero-touch deployment — automated provisioning of new nodes and resources.
export.docx.aime_bullet_6: Resource optimization — dynamic workload balancing and automatic resource rerouting.
export.docx.aime_bullet_7: Policy-based security automation — enforced without manual scripting.
export.docx.aime_intro: AIME by Scale Computing — the AIOps platform for intelligent infrastructure. AIME is the artificial-intelligence orchestration and management functionality that powers the SC//HyperCore virtualization suite. Acting as a digital twin — a hand-built model of the environment the cluster runs in — it continuously thinks about the state the system is in, modelling the hardware, cluster operations, and surrounding environment so SC//HyperCore can handle day-to-day operational and maintenance tasks automatically. AIME monitors for security, hardware, and software errors, remediates issues where possible, and identifies root causes to minimize impact when automatic repair isn't feasible.
export.docx.assumption_1: Sized for N-1 resilience ({clusters}); usable capacity reflects RF2 mirroring.
export.docx.assumption_2: vCPU:core ratio {ratio}:1; OS overhead and growth/snapshot reserve included.
export.docx.assumption_3: No LAG — networking is active/passive failover across two switches.
export.docx.benchmark_disclaimer: Disclaimer: benchmark data is externally sourced (public SPEC and PassMark results); provided for guidance only — no rights can be derived from these figures.
export.docx.benchmark_should_deliver: In a benchmark, this should deliver
export.docx.heat_bullet_1: Per-disk flash priority — configurable flash allocation at the individual virtual-disk level through an easy-to-use slide bar in the HyperCore UI.
export.docx.heat_bullet_2: Intelligent data placement — data-block priority based on block I/O heat mapping assessed from historical information.
export.docx.heat_bullet_3: Automatic warm-up — new writes are assigned to the flash tier until SCRIBE can accurately assess their activity.
export.docx.heat_bullet_4: Exponential priority scale — the 0–11 scale is exponential; raising a virtual disk from 4 to 5 doubles its priority for flash placement.
export.docx.heat_bullet_5: Flexible tiering — setting 0 keeps static data off flash, while setting 11 multiplies flash priority by an order of magnitude.
export.docx.heat_intro: Optimizing storage efficiency with HEAT. In flash-equipped SC//HyperCore nodes, HyperCore Enhanced Automated Tiering (HEAT) meters real-time IOPS for each virtual disk and intelligently places data blocks across the flash and spinning tiers based on block I/O heat mapping assessed from historical activity — striking the correct balance of IOPS efficiency across virtual disks and VMs.
export.docx.mgmt_bullet_1: Single-pane management — compute, storage, networking, and VMs are administered from the built-in HyperCore web interface; no separate storage console or vCenter-equivalent.
export.docx.mgmt_bullet_2: SC Fleet Manager — cloud-based monitoring, alerting, and fleet-wide management across clusters and sites from one dashboard.
export.docx.mgmt_bullet_3: Self-healing storage — RF2 mirroring rebuilds automatically on a disk or node failure, with no administrator intervention.
export.docx.mgmt_bullet_4: Non-disruptive operations — rolling updates and node additions complete with VMs running; scale out by adding a node, no forklift upgrades.
export.docx.mgmt_bullet_5: Built-in data protection — native VM snapshots and replication for backup and DR without third-party software.
export.docx.mgmt_operations_intro: Scale Computing HyperCore runs the hypervisor, storage, and orchestration as a single integrated fabric — managed from one interface with no external SAN, no separate hypervisor licensing, and no specialist virtualization administration required.
export.docx.mgmt_overview_intro: This proposal consolidates the current {platform} environment ({hosts} hosts, {vms} active VMs, {used_tb} TB in use) onto a Scale Computing HyperCore cluster. We recommend a {nodes} {model} cluster delivering {usable_tb} TB usable capacity and {cores} CPU cores, engineered for N-1 resilience so a complete node can fail without service interruption. The configuration absorbs {years}-year growth at {growth}% annually while maintaining a {ratio}:1 vCPU-to-core ratio.
export.docx.passmark_note: Figures marked PassMark are converted to the SPECrate scale (~0.00386 per CPU Mark, roughly ±20%).
export.docx.product_intro: Scale Computing SC//HyperCore simplifies your systems and moves beyond traditional IT silos. The award-winning, self-healing platform identifies, reduces, and corrects problems in real-time — making application uptime easier for IT to manage and more affordable to run. Its lightweight, all-in-one architecture eliminates the need to combine separate virtualization software, disaster recovery software, servers, and shared storage from different vendors, deploying fully integrated, highly available virtualization right out of the box. Designed to scale as the business grows without downtime, disruption, or rigid hardware requirements, it lets teams spend less time on infrastructure maintenance and more on strategic projects.
export.docx.rationale_bar_legend: Each bar is 100% of the full cluster: solid = today's load, light hatch = growth + snapshot reserve the workload is sized to, dark hatch = HA failover capacity held back so the cluster still meets the workload with one node down (N-1).
export.docx.rationale_compute: Determined by CPU performance — this cluster delivers {achieved}% of your current environment's compute demand (rated throughput scaled to {util}% measured peak utilization, grown to the horizon); the node count was raised to clear that floor.
export.docx.rationale_cpu: Determined by CPU — {vcpus} vCPUs ÷ {ratio}:1 overcommit = {required} cores required, vs {achieved} usable cores available at N-1 ({headroom}% headroom).
export.docx.rationale_ram_storage: Determined by {resource} — {required} {unit} required, vs {achieved} {unit} available at N-1 ({headroom}% headroom).
export.docx.scribe_bullet_1: Performance — hypervisor-integrated block storage eliminates file-system latency, disk-partition misalignment, and snapshot delta-file merging.
export.docx.scribe_bullet_2: Simplified management — complex storage management tasks are abstracted away for automated storage with minimal manual intervention.
export.docx.scribe_bullet_3: Reliability — avoiding intermediary file-system abstractions minimizes the risk of data corruption or performance degradation.
export.docx.scribe_bullet_4: Native snapshots — fast, native snapshots with no merge penalties or performance hit.
export.docx.scribe_bullet_5: Storage efficiency — purpose-built for virtualized workloads with zero alignment issues.
export.docx.scribe_bullet_6: Effortless scale — simply add nodes; SCRIBE scales storage automatically with no downtime.
export.docx.scribe_intro: Efficiency redefined. The Scale Computing Reliable Independent Block Engine (SCRIBE) is a critical software component of the SC//HyperCore virtualization suite — an enterprise-class, clustered, block storage layer purpose-built to be consumed directly by the KVM-based SC//HyperCore hypervisor. By interfacing directly with the hypervisor rather than repurposing a traditional file system, SCRIBE eliminates the performance bottlenecks, latency, and alignment issues associated with repurposed file systems.
export.docx.title: Infrastructure Sizing Proposal

export.net.legend_no_lag: No LAG — active/passive failover across switches

export.pptx.specrate_explainer: SPECrate2017_int measures total CPU throughput across all cores — the right yardstick for consolidating VMs. Newer CPUs do more work per core, so a smaller new cluster can comfortably outperform a larger old one.
